_note: Cyberspace databases. ICE strength + matrix coords are the real values from the original (via Javamancer); the content prose is our own.

databases.name: Free Matrix
databases.content: A scrappy open node, run by cowboys for cowboys. Low ICE, a few free software samples, and a board full of people bragging about runs they never made. A good place to cut your teeth.

databases.name: Consumer Review
databases.content: A consumer-ratings node, low-security and chatty. Reviews scroll past, most of them paid for. Buried in the noise: a live comlink code, and a hint that the real money hides a zone deeper.

databases.name: Chiba City Tactical Police
databases.content: The tac-police core, all warrants and watchlists. Your own name scrolls past, flagged 'person of interest, do not approach unarmed.' Charming. There are clean codes here worth lifting, if your nerve holds.

databases.name: Bank Gemeinschaft
databases.content: A Swiss-orbital vault buried deep in the matrix. Anonymous money, anonymous owners, anonymous ICE that doesn't bother to announce itself before it tries to kill you.

databases.name: Bank of Berne
databases.content: The Bank of Berne's core, packed in layers of lethal ICE and watched by an AI that calls itself Gold. The accounts here have more zeros than sense. Take what you can carry and get out before Gold feels the draft.

databases.name: Tessier-Ashpool Core
databases.content: The oldest, coldest ice in the matrix, wound around something that has been waiting for you since the day you first jacked in.
